picks.rationale: Left-aligned headline with badge accent + two CTAs; right grid can be swapped to a lead-form card, matching the desired hero layout.
picks.notes: Replace the right-side image grid with a 3-field lead-capture card. Swap PhoneCall icon color to navy, use Recoleta for h1. Strip the Badge 'We're live!' or reuse for the 1-2 hr response-time promise.

picks.rationale: Cleaner split hero — left copy + CTAs, right full-bleed image slot. Good alternative if lead form should live lower in the section.
picks.notes: Use this if we keep the hero visual-first and push the form to a sticky sidebar or below-the-fold card.

picks.rationale: Thin sticky top bar with wordmark left, inline nav items, and button CTAs — converts cleanly to a phone-pill CTA on the right. Full-width with scroll-triggered backdrop blur fits grounded/tactile aesthetic.
picks.notes: Replace 'Sign In/Get Started' with a single navy phone-pill CTA (e.g., '(847) XXX-XXXX'). Keep the wordmark slot for Torvik logo. Disable the backdrop-blur if it reads too techy — a solid cream background with 1px border is more on-brief.

picks.rationale: Compact rounded floating nav — alternative if the client prefers a modern floating pill over a full-width bar.
picks.notes: Likely too 'tech-startup' for warm-trades feel; include as fallback only. Avoid if brief drifts toward family-owned trades energy.

picks.rationale: Three-card-per-row grid using shadcn Card with soft shadow and 1px border — maps directly onto a 6-item services layout (2 rows x 3 cols). Icon above heading above copy is ideal for service-card content.
picks.notes: Extend from 3 to 6 cards. Replace Lucide AI/tech icons (Zap, Sparkles) with hauling-appropriate Lucide icons: Truck, Wrench, Leaf, Trash2, Building2, Home. Strip the CardDecorator's grid-dot background — too techy.

picks.rationale: Divided 6-cell grid with hairline borders — very tactile/newspaper feel matches warm-trades aesthetic better than card shadows.
picks.notes: Uses divide-x divide-y border — exactly the tactile 1px border detail called for in the brief. Swap icons to hauling set, use Recoleta for h3.

picks.rationale: Horizontal numbered steps with indicator circles and connector lines — 4-step layout ready-made (Steps.Root count=4). Stacks vertically on mobile via ark-ui's orientation prop.
picks.notes: Recolor indicator to navy #0B2E5B (currently blue-600). Pair each step with a Recoleta heading (Contact / Quote & Schedule / Haul or Demo / Clean & Done) and 1-sentence caption. Consider the Steps-Label variant to inline the title.

picks.rationale: Vertical variant of the same component family — useful for mobile breakpoint or if the section reads better stacked.
picks.notes: Same component; change orientation prop. Keep as mobile fallback only; desktop should remain horizontal.

picks.rationale: Three-column grid with star ratings, quote, avatar + name + role attribution — maps exactly to Torvik's 3 verbatim Google reviews. Soft 1px ring border matches the tactile palette.
picks.notes: Replace 'role' with 'via Google' attribution. Swap Star fill-primary color to the accent light-blue (#6FA8DC) or keep gold stars (amber-500) — gold is acceptable since it's in a star glyph context, not a design accent color. Verify with client if gold stars conflict with the no-yellow rule.

picks.rationale: Alternative card-based testimonial layout with stars and avatar footer — useful if we want 2-col layout with more breathing room per quote.
picks.notes: Testimonials-stars is the stronger default; use this only if reviews are long and need more card real estate.

picks.rationale: shadcn Card + Input + Select + Button form with clean vertical layout. Already supports Select component for the service-type dropdown.
picks.notes: Trim fields to 3: Full Name, Service Type (Select: Junk Removal / Demolition / Hauling / Cleanouts / Other), City. Replace the Country/Region select with Service. Add a '1-2 hr response time' caption below the Submit button. Submit button should be navy.

picks.rationale: Split layout — left column for phone/email/web contact details, right column for the form. Good fit for the /contact page itself (not the hero form).
picks.notes: Use this for the dedicated /contact page. Keep the 3-field version (contact-form slug above) for the hero lead-capture. Swap default Input/Textarea to include Select for service type.

picks.rationale: 5-column layout with description + social icons + link groups + bottom legal bar — exactly the information architecture needed (services, areas, company, legal).
picks.notes: Columns: Services / Service Areas / Company (About, Contact, Reviews) / Legal (Privacy, Terms). Use navy (#0B2E5B) background with cream text. Strip the 'Corporates & Universities' + Newsletter cards — not relevant. Keep phone + email in the description column. Social icons: Facebook, Instagram, Google Business.

picks.rationale: Simpler 5-column grid with socials baked into the first column — lighter-weight alternative if the dedicated 5-col footer feels too heavy for a local business.
picks.notes: Good fallback; the primary footer pick has richer IA. This one is better if Torvik's service pages number only 4-5 rather than 6+.

picks.rationale: Pill-shaped rounded badge with stacked avatars + 'Trusted by X' text — can be adapted into a 4-icon horizontal trust bar (Clock, Star, Leaf, Home) with short text beside each icon.
picks.notes: The component itself is a single pill, not a 4-slot bar. Use it as a design reference for the rounded-full, hairline-border, soft-shadow styling and replicate 4 of them inline. Substitute avatar-stack for Lucide icons (Clock for response time, Star for rating, Leaf for eco, Home for family-owned).

picks.rationale: Centered CTA with large headline, muted-foreground subtitle, and two buttons (primary + outline). Clean section that drops between services/testimonials and footer.
picks.notes: Replace 'Get Started / Book Demo' with 'Get a Free Quote' (primary, navy) and 'Call (847) XXX-XXXX' (outline). Use Recoleta for the h2. Set background to bg-muted (warm cream) or invert to navy bg with cream text for the closing CTA variant.

picks.rationale: Left-aligned two-line heading + subtitle + two compact buttons on a muted bg — alternative if we want the closing CTA to feel more like an inline prompt than a hero-adjacent band.
picks.notes: Use either this or the centered variant; avoid having both on the same page. Chevron+Calendar icons should be swapped for Phone+ArrowRight.

gaps.note: 21st.dev does not offer a strong local-business map component. Build custom with Mapbox GL JS or Leaflet + GeoJSON of the 30 service-area cities in Lake County and NW Cook County. Cheaper alternative: render a styled static SVG of the two-county region with the covered cities highlighted in the accent light-blue (#6FA8DC). Include a simple searchable list of all 30 cities beside the map for SEO and accessibility.
